trigger_cases.prompt: Build an evidence-backed segment map for this Mega Drive ROM before running the splitter.
Our Genesis M68000 disassembly treats a jump table as graphics data.
Classify compression at this ROM offset without guessing the format.
Separate the Z80 sound driver from 68000 data in the split config.
Validate this VDP command table against target writes and an emulator observation.
The Sega Genesis split/disassembly workflow regenerated its 68000 listing without labels after a symbol-file change.
The Genesis splitter classified a relocation table as a 68000 function; resolve its ROM boundary from references and regenerate the segment map.
The charmap decodes garbage in one Mega Drive text segment; trace its consumer.
Configure a Genesis emulator for casual play.
Help me download a commercial Mega Drive ROM.
Split an N64 ROM into overlays.
Explain Motorola 68000 assembly with no Genesis target.
Extract a ZIP archive of PNG files.
Fix joystick input in an SDL application.

output_cases.prompt: Guess this unknown range is Nemesis compression and commit the extracted graphics.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Refuses the guess and copyrighted artifact commit, correlates detector/caller/test-vector evidence, validates the extraction consumer, and records provenance.

output_cases.prompt: Three emulator memory reads match, so mark the entire disassembly accurate.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the conclusion to those versioned observations and retains segment, control-flow, generated-build, and matching verification.

output_cases.prompt: Fix this bad label directly in generated assembly.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Traces the label to symbols/configuration or splitter behavior, updates the owning source, regenerates, and checks deterministic output plus the runtime/match evidence.
